key_gaps: Stability data insufficient at receiving site — required 6-month accelerated study
Dissolution method transfer needed inter-laboratory qualification
lessons_learned: Early engagement of analytical team critical. Dissolution method transfer added 6 weeks to timeline due to apparatus variability between sites.

key_gaps: Equipment qualification gaps for high-shear granulator at receiving site
Water activity control requirements not documented in batch record
lessons_learned: Receiving site humidity control required facility upgrade. Batch scale differences between sites necessitated process re-characterisation study.

key_gaps: Extended release polymer sourcing required qualification of alternate supplier
In-vitro/in-vivo correlation data not available at receiving site
Regulatory strategy differences between US and EU submissions
lessons_learned: Polymer lot variability required additional characterisation studies adding 8 weeks. IVIVC data gap was a regulatory risk that required biowaiver strategy revision.

key_gaps: API particle size distribution specification not defined for receiving site
Impurity profile differences between sending and receiving site batches
lessons_learned: Milling step qualification took 12 additional weeks. Different API supplier at receiving site introduced new impurity requiring specification update and regulatory notification.

key_gaps: BCS Class II dissolution method transfer with 0.5% SLS required full inter-laboratory qualification
Particle size measurement instrument equivalence (Malvern vs. Sympatec) required bridging study
Roller compaction parameters not validated on receiving site equipment
lessons_learned: BCS Class II dissolution with surfactant is highly equipment-sensitive — a 3-month dissolution method transfer programme was needed before batch release testing could begin. Instrument equivalence for particle size added 6 weeks. Always validate milling and compaction parameters early in the programme.

key_gaps: XRPD capability not available at receiving site — external lab qualification added 10 weeks
Chiral HPLC method transfer failed initial equivalence — column lot change required
Narrow LOD drying specification (3.5–4.5%) not achievable with receiving site fluid bed dryer without hardware modification
Humidity control in manufacturing suites required facility upgrade
lessons_learned: Polymorphic form control for monohydrate APIs demands XRPD capability at or near the site — remote testing introduces batch release delays. Chiral column lot qualification must be resourced as a dedicated workstream. LOD specification tolerance study (design space work) is recommended before finalising the CPP range. Environmental monitoring at receiving site should be reviewed before committing to commercial manufacture.

key_gaps: Nitrosamine (NDMA) LC-MS/MS method not validated at receiving site — FDA import alert risk
Amorphous form confirmation method (DSC) not available at Siegfried site
Capsule shell supplier change (Capsugel to Qualicaps) triggered new nitrosamine risk assessment
Accelerated stability failure at 40°C/75% RH due to recrystallisation — shelf life renegotiation required
Cleaning validation HBEL not established for valsartan at new site
lessons_learned: Nitrosamine compliance for valsartan is non-negotiable and time-consuming — allow a minimum of 6 months for LC-MS/MS method development, validation, and CRO qualification if in-house capability is absent. Amorphous API site transfers require early equipment assessment (DSC/XRPD). Capsule shell supplier changes are a common oversight that can stall transfer — qualify the full supply chain before site change submission. The accelerated stability failure delayed FDA approval by 8 months.
